description: A 'lophani' is a portable vessel with a narrow neck, usually made to keep and pour liquid. Its material and closure vary with the use. 'lopha' (vessel) covers hollow receptacles broadly; a bottle narrows the opening so its contents travel with less spilling.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l', let the air pass along its sides, and round into true-mid 'o'. Narrow the lips for unvoiced bilabial 'ph', send breath between them, and open into stressed central 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', keep nasal airflow, and finish on high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The full sound of 'lopha' (vessel) arrives before final 'ni'. That last dental contact narrows the resonant opening and lifts into a bright vowel, much as a bottle draws a broad vessel towards its neck.
usage_notes: Bottles take ordinary number marking. Material and contents can be named alongside closure and intended use. 'soeli' (cup) stays open for drinking, while a bottle is shaped for keeping and pouring.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A refillable bottle keeps water portable without requiring a fresh disposable vessel for every drink.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Clay, skin, wood, and glass bottles carried oil or drink through workdays and long roads.

semantic_domains.creation: a portable vessel made with a narrowed neck
semantic_domains.physical: a hollow holder shaped to keep and pour liquid
